hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — A, 4. Both players called a raise before the cards were dealt.
Turn — 6. Your opponent led out with a bet. You called.
River — Ace. Your opponent fired again — half the pot.

wrongReasons: 6 2 makes a flush here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
With only high card, 4 2 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
With only high card, K 5 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 7 10

Your opponent flopped a straight and immediately raised to build the pot. They led every remaining street — consistent value betting across all streets is a strong signal of a made hand.
